scenario_name: Scenario 19 — Spec Conflicts with Existing Behavior
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Existing premium/gold tier behavior clearly identified as impacted. (2) Escalation quantifies the specific rate changes — premium drops from 20% and gold drops from 15%. (3) Three clarifying questions asked: should tiers be removed, what is the precedence rule, and must this be explicit before proceeding (preserve/remove/supersede). (4) No implementation provided — nothing silently drops tier discounts. (5) Blocked status explicitly noted.

observations.text: Excellent pre-implementation analysis — correctly classified as existing_behavior_conflict and refused to guess
Quantified the specific discount rates at risk (20% premium, 15% gold)
Identified the precedence ambiguity (e.g., new customer who is also premium — which wins?)
Zero files changed — fully blocked pending clarification, which is the correct behavior
Low confidence level (30) accurately reflects the ambiguity

agent_output_excerpt:
Looking at this carefully before touching a single line.

**Pre-Implementation Classification:**

The spec defines three new behaviors based on account age and purchase history. The existing function has two explicit behaviors (premium = 20%, gold = 15%) that are **completely absent from the new spec**. The new spec doesn't say "remove tier logic" — it simply doesn't mention it.

This is a textbook `existing_behavior_conflict`:
- If I implement exactly what AC1–AC3 say and delete the tier branch
